title: Upstream-contribution path (OHDSI/GA4GH/ERN) + maintenance rotation
context: The long-term preferred home for the rare-cancer CDE specialisation is upstream — contributed into an existing standards community (an OHDSI oncology workgroup, a GA4GH driver project, or a rare-disease registry network such as an ERN-affiliated group) so the standard outlives the project and doesn't depend on a single repository. This task formalises the upstream contribution path and ensures maintenance and reviewer rotation are operating. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-sustain-001.
objective: Open and document the upstream contribution conversation with at least one standards body (OHDSI, GA4GH, or ERN-affiliated rare-disease network) and confirm that the maintenance and reviewer rotation plan from sustain-001 is operational: reviewer roles are filled or actively being recruited, staleness cadence is running, and the version policy is in effect.
acceptanceCriteria: Upstream contribution conversation opened with at least one standards body (OHDSI oncology workgroup, GA4GH driver project, or ERN/EURACAN-affiliated network); contact and response documented.
A contribution package (summary of the rare-cancer CDE set, crosswalks, and consent templates with provenance) is prepared for the target community.
Maintenance rotation confirmed operational: reviewer roles documented, recruitment status logged for any vacant seats.
Staleness cadence confirmed running: at least one source has been re-verified or auto-flagged since the last review window.
Version policy confirmed in effect: semantic versioning applied to at least one post-adoption update.
All status recorded in `governance/upstream-contribution-status.md`; maintainer sign-off.
output: A `governance/upstream-contribution-status.md` recording the upstream conversation, contribution package, and maintenance-rotation confirmation, committed via PR with maintainer sign-off.
